examples.title: Basic Function Overloading Example
examples.description: Demonstrates function overloading with different parameter types
examples.explanation: Three overloaded add() functions with different signatures: (int,int), (double,double), (int,int,int). Compiler selects appropriate function based on argument types at compile-time.

examples.title: Function Overloading with Reference Parameters
examples.description: Shows overloading based on reference and const-reference
examples.explanation: Three versions: pass-by-value, pass-by-reference, pass-by-const-reference. Different argument types (lvalue, const lvalue, rvalue) call different functions.

examples.title: Demonstrating Ambiguity in Overloading
examples.description: Shows cases where ambiguity arises and causes compilation error
examples.explanation: Demonstrates ambiguity scenarios: when multiple overloads match equally (like char matching both int and double) or when default arguments create identical signatures. Solution: explicit type casting.

examples.title: Function Overloading for Class Operations
examples.description: Shows practical use of overloading in a Calculator class
examples.explanation: Demonstrates practical class-based function overloading. Same method name 'add' performs different operations based on argument types - integer addition, float addition, triple addition, and string concatenation.
